summary: SKTypeface.FromFamilyName returns default font (Noto Mono) instead of requested font family in Blazor WASM because the WASM sandbox has no access to system fonts.

stepsToReproduce: Create a Blazor WASM app with SKCanvasView
Use SKTypeface.FromFamilyName("Tahoma", SKFontStyle.BoldItalic) to create a typeface
Check FamilyName — always returns "Noto Mono" regardless of the requested family
Text is rendered with Noto Mono instead of the requested font

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: This is a fundamental WASM platform limitation — Skia's font manager in WASM only has access to bundled fonts, not system fonts. This behavior has not changed.

analysis.summary: In WASM, Skia's font manager has no access to browser or system fonts. When SKTypeface.FromFamilyName is called, SkTypeface::MakeFromName delegates to the font manager which can only resolve fonts bundled into the WASM binary. Since only Noto Mono is bundled by default, all family name lookups fall back to it. This is by-design platform behavior, not a SkiaSharp bug.
analysis.rationale: Classified as question (not bug) because the behavior is correct for the WASM platform — the font manager cannot access system fonts from a sandboxed environment. The reporter and community confirmed the workaround of loading fonts via stream. Multiple commenters have validated this approach over 3+ years.

keySignals.interpretation: Default WASM font returned because requested font not available in sandbox

keySignals.text: Skia's font manager is highly limited under wasm so you can't expect a functional font manager. It is better to load fonts explicitly via stream
keySignals.interpretation: Domain expert confirms WASM font manager limitation — expected behavior

keySignals.text: The font is loaded with SKTypeface.FromStream, reading the resource with Reflection. Issue resolved
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter self-resolved using FromStream with embedded resource

keySignals.text: Embedding a font in my WASM Blazor app and loading it via a stream does indeed work
keySignals.interpretation: Workaround confirmed by another user in Jan 2025

codeInvestigation.finding: FromFamilyName delegates to sk_typeface_create_from_name which calls SkTypeface::MakeFromName — this uses the platform font manager, which on WASM only knows about bundled fonts
C API directly calls SkTypeface::MakeFromName(familyName, style) — no special WASM handling, font manager fallback returns default typeface when family not found
FromStream, FromData, and FromFile provide alternative font loading that bypasses the font manager — these work on WASM because they load font data directly

workarounds: Use SKTypeface.FromStream() with an embedded resource font file
Use SKTypeface.FromData() with font data fetched via HttpClient or JS interop
Register fonts as a singleton service to avoid reloading on each paint

resolution.hypothesis: WASM sandbox prevents access to system fonts. Skia's font manager only resolves bundled fonts (Noto Mono), so FromFamilyName always returns the default. Fonts must be loaded explicitly via stream or data.

proposals.title: Load font from embedded resource
proposals.description: Embed the .ttf font file as an embedded resource in the Blazor project and load it using SKTypeface.FromStream at startup. Register the typeface as a singleton service for reuse.

proposals.title: Fetch font via HttpClient
proposals.description: Download the font file from a web service using HttpClient and load it via SKTypeface.FromData. This avoids bloating the assembly and allows browser caching. Ensure CORS headers are configured if fetching from an external server.

resolution.recommendedReason: Avoids bloating the WASM assembly size, allows browser HTTP caching, and supports dynamic font selection. The embedded resource approach is simpler but increases app download size.

actionability.reason: WASM sandbox limitation — FromFamilyName cannot access system fonts by design. Workaround is well-established and confirmed by reporter and community.

actions.description: Apply question, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Post explanation of WASM font limitation with workaround code
